response: Ensemble models run many simulations. Each run uses slightly different starting data. This approach helps forecasters see probability of outcomes. For a hurricane, paths vary. Data assimilation feeds new info into each model run. Small errors grow fast in weather. One model might miss a shift. Many models show a range. If most agree, confidence rises. If they spread out, uncertainty grows. This method reduces false alarms. It also lowers missed events. Forecasters use this to warn people. Safety improves with better guidance. The system handles chaos well. No single guess works best. A group of guesses wins. We see patterns in spread. This helps plan for storms. The process needs huge computing power.
